signals.title: NZD/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme
signals.text: RBNZ 2.5%. Despite NZD COT LF net short -27,675 (18% long), the pair shows a 2.137% G10 spread advantage. The extreme spec short positioning vs a hiking CB creates a high-conviction short-squeeze regime as USD hawkish bets wane. NZD outperformance is sustained despite a -0.58% real rate and 125bp OIS carry deficit vs USD.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry vs Intervention Risk + Vol Premium
signals.text: USD/JPY at 162.11 remains bid on the +265bp SOFR-TONA OIS carry advantage, holding well above the 160.00 psychological intervention threshold. Options markets are pricing significant tail risk with USD/JPY IV at 7.2% vs HV30 of 3.5% (VRP +3.6%), while 25d RR at -1.39 confirms a moderate put skew as traders hedge for BoJ action. JPY COT LF net short -104,231 (29.1% long) reflects a crowded carry trade vulnerable to verbal or physical intervention.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Positioning + Vol + Retail Divergence
signals.text: GBP/USD at 1.3526 shows institutional and retail divergence; COT LF net long +17,228 (59% long) vs retail 75% short. The options market is pricing a massive +4.7% Vol Risk Premium (IV 11.0% vs HV30 6.3%), signaling extreme hedging demand despite a neutral -10bp OIS carry spread. Negative CESI (-6 net misses) suggests macro data is failing to support the move, making the current 1W +0.82% rally susceptible to a long-unwind if BoE hold bias shifts dovish.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Technical Resistance + Macro Headwinds
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1471 is testing the range top at 1.1485. While the ECB remains in a hiking cycle (2.25%), the +145bp OIS carry advantage favors USD. EUR CESI at -15 (strong negative) indicates persistent data misses, contradicting the 1W +0.32% price recovery. COT positioning remains net short (lev_net -66,057), and with 25d RR at -0.45, the options market maintains a mild put skew, suggesting the 1.1485 level will act as a pivot for the next leg lower.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Correlation Break + Positioning Extreme
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.4046 exhibits a significant correlation break vs WTI Oil (corr -0.926 vs norm -0.003, z=-1.96σ), as CAD positioning hits a bearish extreme (COT long% 22.6%, lev_net -87,846). The +135bp OIS carry advantage for USD and neutral 25d RR (+0.02) suggest the pair is decoupling from energy prices to follow the Fed-BoC policy divergence. BoC 2.25%.
